[IMAGE:0]Midnight is just a few minutes away, and so it's time to launch a new Magic Monday. Ask me anything about occultism, and with certain exceptions noted below, any question received by midnight Monday Eastern time will get an answer. Please note:  Any question or comment received after then will not get an answer, and in fact will just be deleted.  If you're in a hurry, or suspect you may be the 143,916th person to ask a question, please check out the very rough version 1.1 of The Magic Monday FAQ here. 

Also: I will not be putting through or answering any more questions about practicing magic around children. I've answered those in simple declarative sentences in the FAQ. If you read the FAQ and don't think your question has been answered, read it again. If that doesn't help, consider remedial reading classes; yes, it really is as simple and straightforward as the FAQ says.  And further:  I've decided that questions about getting goodies from spirits are also permanently off topic here. The point of occultism is to develop your own capacities, not to try to bully or wheedle other beings into doing things for you. I've discussed this in a post on my blog.

The image? I field a lot of questions about my books these days, so I've decided to do little capsule summaries of them here, one per week. The book above was my forty-ninth published book, my seventh published novel, and the book in which The Weird of Hali really found its rhythm. It was harder to write than the two earlier volumes in my para-Lovecraftian series; Innsmouth practically wrote itself, and Kingsport took a little more work but I never had to struggle to see where it was going. Chorazin began smoothly, but once the action reached the little town of Chorazin in the shadow of Elk Hill, it took me repeated drafts to get things to come out right. Still, I think the result was as good as anything in the series, and it brought in themes and characters who turned out to be central to later books. You can order a copy here if you live in the US and here if you live elsewhere.

Buy Me A Coffee

Ko-Fi

You can use either of the links above to access my online tip jar; Buymeacoffee is good for small tips, Ko-Fi is better for larger ones. comments.title: Background noise or something worse?
comments.content:
Hi JMG, are there any practices you would recommend for recognizing malign spiritual influences in different settings and situations? I find myself in many different social environments in the course of my personal and professional lives, and I've observed rising interest in the grubbier side of the occult among some people as well as influences like Covid mania that are having negative effects. Among other things, I've seen people who led normal lives for a long time go off the rails mentally and drop out of their old circles, and their stories have had consistent elements leading me to believe that something not entirely mundane may be going on.

It's a no-brainer to stay away from anyone openly professing or displaying involvement with noxious forces, but if someone doesn't do this I'm not exactly planning to ask them if they've picked up a habit of making offerings to evil spirits. I often encounter hippie types and sundry spiritual dabblers in my travels and I've read accounts including yours of such subcultures being ground zero for demonolatry and related practices. The polluted state of the astral leads me to wonder whether some bad vibes I pick up are specific to a place or a person or just part of background noise. Is there any method you'd recommend for making the distinction between "definitely avoid this place or activity" and "there's some muck floating around the subtle planes but not particularly so in this spot"?

Thanks for your perspective and for making these posts.

replies.title: Re: Background noise or something worse?
replies.content:
The first thing I'd suggest is that you should take up a daily banishing ritual of some sort if you haven't done so already. As this cleanses your aura, people who engage in the grubbier sort of magic will tend to avoid you, and noxious energies will have no effect on you.

The second thing I'd suggest is learning some form of divination, and getting good enough at it that you can ask such questions of it and get clear answers. This is very useful for sorting out what you're experiencing.

replies.title: Re: Background noise or something worse?
replies.content: Thanks, I protect myself with banishing rituals, are there any particular divination forms you'd recommend? I've mostly practiced three-card tarot draws.

replies.title: Re: Background noise or something worse?
replies.content: Divination is a very personal matter -- what works for one person may or may not work for another. Tarot is certainly an option, or you might explore other methods to see if any of them work better for you.

replies.title: Re: Background noise or something worse?
replies.content: One of the things I really liked reading in your edition of The Doors of Tarot by John Gilbert was his remark that, if you have been doing a banishing ritual regularly, you are yourself already a "spiritual space". Just that seed idea is enormously helpful all around in this work.

replies.title: Re: Background noise or something worse?
replies.content:
Not JMG,

I've noticed that focusing on avoiding evil tends to set my mind to looking for evil I might need to avoid, and when this happens I tend to find it because I'm looking for it, which is the opposite of what I wanted to do in the first place. I've also noticed its a lot easier to figure out what I don't want than it is to decide what I want, so its easy for avoidance of evil to turn into avoidance of seeking the good, which is bad.

Instead of focusing on avoiding evil, I would suggest focusing on the positive and the good that you want more of in your life. By aligning yourself with what you want, you'll get more of it and naturally avoid being dragged into what you don't want.

replies.title: Re: Background noise or something worse?
replies.content:
Brings to mind a Sufi saying:

'Spend too much time looking at crooked things, and you might then not be able to recognise the straight!'

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
I've tried to build a world-understanding that synthesizes the constraints from a bunch of observations, including reports about apportations (as well as apparitions and poltergeist phenomena). I have a concrete hypothesis for where the object "goes", but it requires some concepts that fall out of an underlying cosmological theory that I'm not sure how to give a handle for. Maybe I can introduce the cosmological theory as being a hypothesis about certain kinds of processes that might transform the conditions experienced by a given mass of consciousness, so that those conditions could go from being in practice mostly equivalent to idealism (i.e. everything is mostly-purpose-directed thoughts in an ever-shifting mind), to being in practice mostly equivalent to materialism (i.e. everything is mostly-incapable-of-foresight patterns of matter in a regular spacetime).

The theory is: maybe our experienced universe is an accretion and concretion of many, many partial drafts, and sub-scenarios, and options, and provisionally-canonized automatisms, and tendencies toward possible combinations of experiences. And maybe these dreamstuff puzzle pieces or timeline fragments are assembled, tuned, and mutually distilled together to create one or more timelines to be consciously experienced. ("Mutually distilled" -- is there a better alchemical name for such operations?) In the decisions made as part of this construction process, there would be one eye toward a theoretical ideal of complete consistency, but another eye toward other priorities.

(To give some points of reference for this hypothesis, there are parts of it that can be put in correspondence with parts of the cosmological training-imagery given by Dion Fortune in "The Cosmic Doctrine". The process of building out possible automatisms, and picking candidate structures of canonicality for possible combinations of automatisms, might correspond to the work of the Lords of Flame. And one reason to care about a theoretical ideal of complete consistency might be for the sake of suitability of the resulting tendencies of experience for use in an "initiation of the nadir". I usually suppose that the planes partly correspond to different tiers of a drafting process for eventually-concretized experienced timelines.)

(CONTENT WARNING: persons who expect to spend much time close to the edge of tipping over into psychosis at some point in the future, and who haven't practiced the experience of worldview pluralism or in some other way cultivated their endurance for having unconsidered-but-psychologically-load-bearing certainties shaken, might want to stop reading past this line, because a bit of an infohazard is coming up soon. I've tried to use the Dreamwidth "details" and "summary" drop-down tags to put a speed bump in the way of just going on passively reading here. JMG, as always, feel free to cut here or anywhere)

click to open if it's safe
The existence and underlying nature of "spontaneous apportations" would mostly arise, not from any form of consistency organically internal to the resulting idealized timeline(s) themselves, but instead from certain recurring patterns of limited inconsistency that would have tended to come up a lot as part of a construction process immediately surrounding those timelines. In different drafts of different phases of a given event, an object might be present or absent in one location or another. During the process where these drafts are assembled and the fitnesses of each candidate possible completed event are weighed, the consistency of any given object's presence or location through the course of the event might at first take a back seat to other more immediate priorities. In events whose consistency doesn't matter as much, these inconsistencies might never be resolved. (The tradeoffs here might be likened to the tradeoffs faced by a film producer deciding how many staff to hire for the continuity supervisor's department, to make sure continuity of costumes, weather, and within-scene location will be maintained across the different shots and scenes of the finished film. Sometimes continuity errors just aren't worth the extra work it would have taken to reliably prevent them.)

And maybe there are beings who participate in the experienced-timeline-concretion-process in something of the same way that full experiencers do, but who stay at some level of remove from full material realization (whether or not they also stay at some level of remove from a full state of some other form of maya adapted to their condition), because those beings had other priorities that would have been incompatible with making themselves able to withstand the rigors of the usual, canonical amount of consistency. (E.g. the aforementioned elves.) Maybe such beings would have long since developed habits and practices that depended on certain forms of inconsistency that seemed natural at their level. So, even though they lived within a process that was mostly organized around an idealized principle of consistent physical histories, their everyday lived-world would include commonplace use of particular patterns of violation of that consistency, that were themselves still mostly-consistent on some other level. Apportations might be such a pattern. Sometimes, such a being might have reason to power such an inconsistency to the level where it becomes "canon" even for materially realized beings, if other consistency priorities permit. (These priorities might be more permissive where uninvolved third parties can more readily interpret the inconsistency as caused by a memory or perception failure, or by a missed hypothesis such as someone having an item get caught on their clothes while they were asleep.)

Where does an apported object "go"? How does it "travel"? Maybe it's like where a lamp flame "goes" before you notice it has been blown out and relight an equivalent flame (i.e. you push the lamp back into the same state-space attractor basin of "currently lit" that it was previously in) -- or where a knot in a slippery hose "goes" if you memorize it, untie it, and retie the same knot somewhere else. It went into your practice of making sure that there would eventually be another lamp flame or knot like that. An apported object's existence might never have been solidly supported in the ironclad way you thought it was. What supports the object's existence might instead be the constructors' memory practices, and their only-mostly-ironclad reasons for maintaining consistency of physical locations and details.

I suspect that mass apparitions (including some UFOs) work on a similar principle. In a mass apparition, multiple people experience overlaid sensation-of-physical-object-like experiences having shared content, even though the physical object "isn't" "present", and different people's experiences follow the shared content with different degrees of precision or with different senses. How might that work?

Beings adjacent to physical realization might have a practice of communicating sensory imagery to each other by picking controlled "what-if" scenarios about sensory experiences that physically realized people could hypothetically have, and then freely postulating sensory details within those scenarios. The "what-if" scenarios are normally never intended to be properly realized; it's just a convenient convention afforded by the existing infrastructure for constructing timelines. However, this leads into a natural affordance for the beings to communicate such sensory experiences to physically realized beings: they can just empower the "what-if" in a controlled inconsistent way, to affect sensory experiences in much the same way that the timeline-construction mechanism was originally designed to support. Different people would receive the sensory experiences with different degrees of precision, perhaps e.g. because they would be at different depths in the nadir and their sense experiences would have different built-out default habits of reception, making it cheaper or costlier to impart sensory contents flagged with different degrees of realistic consistency. (I think of the anecdote of a meditation practitioner who went to Tibet, and said it was a decade before she started seeing the dragons in the sky.)

The unsettling implication is that apportations and apparitions might be a place where the underlying structure of our material universe as a cumulatively constructed sort-of-"illusion" breaks through. The solidity of materiality would be just a matter of how strong the system's internal incentives are to keep the illusion as it is. One has a queer apprehension that, if an apparition just somehow became powered strongly enough, maybe canon would twist around and the apparition would become "permanently", canonically physically real...?

The feeling is like if you lived in a society built so that the laws could invariably be enforced by unseen police, no matter what social maneuvers anyone around a potential law-violation did to try to make the law not get enforced, so that you got the idea that the law was completely independent of anyone's attitudes. Imagine if you then witnessed a series of blatant gubernatorial pardons, which could only have resulted from someone's attitudes. So you would realize that the law enforcement was always being constructed as a series of purposeful decisions after all; it would just be that there was a reason that the attitudes behind the decisions had previously been unvarying.

Maybe if too many things like apportations and apparitions were allowed in a careless way, there would be a slippery slope and ... I'm not sure how to express this part, but in software engineering you might call it a problem of "technical debt" coming to call. In sociology you might call it a breakdown of norms. (There's a particular tangent here of major interest to me, but this comment is already long. The short version of the tangent is: given this situation, is there a form of restorative meta-consistency that it would be virtuous to strive toward?)

I don't have a similarly compelling hypothesis about poltergeist phenomena. Now that I think about it, though, I guess poltergeists do usually move objects in ways in which someone present might plausibly have physically moved the objects in a different, ultimately non-canonized (but perhaps we could say "deuterocanonical"?) timeline-fragment.

comments.title: First Pass at Saying the Heathen Rosary and Question About Paired Rulers of the Currents of Nwyfre
comments.content:
Good Evening,

As we're at last getting some fall-ish weather down here in my corner of the humid subtropical south of the US, I hope that those of you in the northern hemisphere are also enjoying the settling in of autumn.

To Share: Now that I have enough of the prayers for the Heathen Rosary to put together into a coherent practice, I've shared that draft form in case anyone else wants to give it a shot: https://jpowellrussell.com/#first_pass_at_saying_the_heathen_rosary

To Ask: As a Heathen following Druid teachings for the time being, I've continued to be unsatisfied with the discrepancy between the Heathen worship of the Sun as a Goddess and the Moon as a God, compared with the respectively masculine and feminine attributions given in most other western occult/religious traditions. As I was meditating my way through the Fifth Grade of the Dolmen Arch course, though, I had a thought for one possible way to reconcile these, and I wondered what you (JMG) and others here thought: the Druid Philosophy Triads in this grade talk about how the three great families of Welsh myth, the Plant Annwn, Plant Don, and Plant Llyr, once had single, male rulers, but have in this age of the world moved to having male/female pairs of rulers. These three families are associated with the Telluric, Solar, and Lunar currents. It occurred to me that one possible (partial?) solution to my conundrum is that different religious traditions have given more weight to the male or female ruler of these current, and by that, associated that ruler with the source of that current. To get concrete, if this hypothesis is correct, the Germanic folk, for whatever reason, picked up more on the female ruler of the Solar Current and worship Her as the Sun herself, Sonne/Sunna/Sol, and the male ruler of the Lunar Current, and worship Him as the Moon Himself, Mone/Mani. Of course, this hypothesis doesn't account for whatever gender the celestial bodies and the earth might (or might not) have in and of themselves, and instead sidesteps that question by looking at one of the main ways we experience those bodies spiritually (through the currents that emanate from them). Any thoughts on whether this is worth further research and meditation or is barking up the wrong tree?

Thank you again, JMG and everyone here!

My blessings to all who welcome them,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: First Pass at Saying the Heathen Rosary and Question About Paired Rulers of the Currents of Nwyf
replies.content:
Oh, anything is worth further research and meditation. I've seen people extract impressive spiritual insights from old Thundarr the Barbarian reruns. I would comment as follows:

1) I'm far from sure that planets have genders at all, and if they do, they needn't be anything like the genders that terrestrial multicellular organisms have.

2) Sun gods and moon goddesses are nothing like as universal as some writers like to pretend. In Japan the sun is a goddess and the moon a god. In Egypt the moon is a god -- that's one of Thoth's roles -- and the sun is sometimes male, in the form of Ra, and sometimes female, in the form of Sekhmet. Among the Native American peoples of the Puget Sound area where I grew up, the sun and moon are brothers, and the sun's the younger, weaker brother, since he just has to light up the day while the moon has to light up the night!

3) I sometimes wonder if the difference is simply that in northern climates, where the sun is so often veiled in clouds, it makes more sense culturally to see it as a shy maiden, whereas in southern climates, where the sun blazes down bright and fierce, it makes more sense culturally to see it as a brash young man.

replies.title: RE: #2
replies.content:
I've wondered if it's something like a category error to assign to the celestial bodies the rank of god/goddess. I mean, I think for all sense and purposes, the planets and the lights, in comparison to us, might as well be the infinite and ultimate powers, but they're still within the influence of the gods.

The antique concept of beings called "archons," who play divinities, but aren't, seems like it could be repurposed to be less... hostile towards the celestial powers on our end.

Depending on the relationship a mortal has with the god, couldn't, for example, Ares not just be Martial (of Mars) Ares, but maybe Venusian Ares, or Jovian, Mercurial, Saturnine, Lunar, etc.? In Kabbalistic terms, is it possible that every deity is an infinitely unique Ein manifesting through the whole Tree of Life?

replies.title: Re: #2
replies.content:
1) It depends on what you mean by those much-vexed words "god" and "goddess." Equivalent terms in various traditions are used in wildly different ways. I've considered reviving the term Oyarsa (plural Oyarses), which C.S. Lewis borrowed from the 11th-century Platonist Bernard Sylvester, for the great planetary beings.

2) Depends on their placement in the person's natal horoscope, I suppose.

replies.title: Re: First Pass at Saying the Heathen Rosary and Question About Paired Rulers of the Currents of Nwyf
replies.content: And then there is the south Slavic language region, where moon is both female “Luna” and male “Mesec”.

replies.title: Re: First Pass at Saying the Heathen Rosary and Question About Paired Rulers of the Currents of Nwyf
replies.content:
Along the lines of what JMG said: the Northern Sun is warm and life-giving, nurturing, and longed-for during the long, cold, dark winters. The Mediterranean / Southern Moon is sultry, beautiful, seductive, warm, breath-taking. Both of these sets of features are often considered feminine.

The Mediterranean / Southern Sun is bold, fierce, unrelenting, pounding. The Northern Moon is austere, distant, cold. These traits are often considered masculine.

I've spent years living in the Mediterranean area, and other years in Northern US and Europe, and I can definitely see why the different cultures view Sun and Moon differently!

Winifred

replies.content:
Responding more specifically to Jeff's question: are the Solar and Lunar currents felt the same way, everywhere and everywhen on Earth? Or does it happen that Earth's atmosphere, the angle at which the Solar / Lunar rays strike each spot on Earth, and other Earth features like geomagnetism cause slight changes in the nature of these rays that are different at different points and times on the Earth? So that it would make sense for humans to interpret these energies as predominantly 'masculine or feminine' depending on where and when they are experienced?

Winifred

Cheers,
Jeff

replies.title: Re: First Pass at Saying the Heathen Rosary and Question About Paired Rulers of the Currents of Nwyf
replies.content: I can definitely see the distinction in my own climate (southern US), though I have no idea how the natives personified them.  The Sun is harsh, unyielding, unbearable, almost Saturnine; one longs for the relative coolness of night and for the nights, presided over by the much more striking Moon, to grow longer as the summer ebbs into fall.

replies.title: Re: First Pass at Saying the Heathen Rosary and Question About Paired Rulers of the Currents of Nwyf
replies.content:
Jeff, your Heathen Rosary is truly amazing! Perhaps another name for it could be "The Tally of the Bedes" or "Tallying the Bedes"? And you mentioned 'ninesomes,' perhaps that could be 'ninefolds'?

In the Heathen soul lore study groups that I run I suggest that people make or obtain 'soul-tokens' which can take the form of beads on a tally-string like yours, braids, jewelry, carvings, etc, used for meditation and other workings as the students wish. During the study session for each soul I also offer an initiation ceremony (we do this online, all participating) for each soul and soul-token.

But yours is obviously more elaborate; I applaud the creativity, effort, and dedication you put into it!

Hail the Holy Ones!
Winifred

replies.title: Re: First Pass at Saying the Heathen Rosary and Question About Paired Rulers of the Currents of Nwyf
replies.content:
Thank you so much for your kind words, from you, that's high praise indeed.

"Ninefolds" might just work, I'll have to give it some thought. The same for "Tally of the Bedes" or "Tallying the Bedes," as I've been looking for the right balance of acknowledging the clear model of the Rosary, while also wanting an Old English-derived name for the whole thing after going to so much trouble to write everything else that way!

And that's very interesting on the "soul-tokens." I'm very fond of using beads as aids to meditation (I have a bracelet for the Elder Futhark, which was the very first thing I ever made with beads), and I suspect I'll have to rein myself in from making far too many to foster any kind of depth with each of them.

Thank you again, I'm in the home stretch now - one more bede for the closing, and then to work out what the "riddles" (mysteries) will be to contemplate while saying (or "telling") the bedes.

Hail the Holy Ones indeed!
Jeff

comments.title: Thanks for astrology help with choosing a weapon material and about spiritualy charging a weapon
comments.content:
About a year ago, I asked JMG on this forum about the spiritual implications of the choice between an aluminum alloy or steel frame gun. JMG advised me to ask people to cast my chart and see what is more favorable in my case, and two kind souls were willing to help - a_friend and weilong and I'd like to thank them for taking the time and effort, and well as JMG for making this suggestion in the first place. Both agreed aluminum is slightly more favorable, and I ended up factoring this into my decision. My question for JMG and anyone else willing to chime in is: how would one go about "charging" my gun with positive vibes? Any advice offered will be appreciated.

In case JMG takes interest as I know he often does - I do not know how to do any magic, I was wondering more in line if the lay person could still do anything to start a good relationship with this weapon I hope will accompany me in the decades to come.

replies.title: Re: Thanks for astrology help with choosing a weapon material and about spiritualy charging a weapon
replies.content: If you don't have any magical training, my advice on charging it -- which presupposes magic -- won't help you. Quite possibly someone else will have a suggestion.

replies.title: Re: Thanks for astrology help with choosing a weapon material and about spiritualy charging a weapon
replies.content:
I remember that in the Middle Ages, a squire on the eve of being knighted had his sword laid upon the altar of the local church and he was encouraged to kneel in armor praying over his weapon and contemplating the responsibilities he is about to take up the entire night before his knighting ceremony. In the ceremony itself he would swear an oath describing why he was taking the sword, for what purpose, and with what duties and responsibilities to whom.

As knights were unlikely to have rigorous magical training, this practice may be worth adapting for your needs. If you have no relationship with any particular deity, an extended meditation on what you're doing taking up the weapon and your subsequent responsibilities towards the weapon, yourself, and your community may work equally well. If an all night vigil is a bit much, consider several sessions of contemplation rather than just the one. Regardless, if you swear any oath or otherwise understand yourself to have a responsibility arising from owning or carrying a weapon.

To cement your contemplation, consider concluding by carrying out your responsibilities in some way - cleaning/maintaining it, refamiliarizing yourself with your local regulations, carrying it with you the next time you're out if you can legally and prudently do so, etc.

replies.title: Re: Thanks for astrology help with choosing a weapon material and about spiritualy charging a weapon
replies.content:
Fellow (relatively) new gun owner here! You might want to check out regular commenter Milkyway’s site, The Hidden Things. She has a basics of blessings three-part series on how to bless. It’s aimed (pun!) at non-magicians.

I always bless my .22 after cleaning it, generally once a month. The jury is out on whether it’s the regular cleaning or the blessing that contributes most to its jam-free functioning. As you probably know, .22s are notoriously finicky. Not mine. Not any more.

OtterGirl

replies.title: Re: Thanks for astrology help with choosing a weapon material and about spiritualy charging a weapon
replies.content:
The traditional baseline for this would be to give the gun a name - most men give a female name, but that's by no means required. The idea is to foster a relationship, rather than merely seeing the gun as an inert tool, and so to treat her/him (rather than it) with respect and to be open to her/him having quirks of "personality" that you can respond to and work with.

Besides that, I rather like anonymous's suggestion to spend some time meditating on what this gun means to you, especially the responsibilities that come with her/him. If you'd like to go further on this, a short charm, prayer, or blessing that you say whenever you take up the gun, whether to clean, carry, fire, or whatever, and/or one whenever you put him or her away might be fitting.

Hope this helps!
Jeff

comments.title: Languages and Sight-Reading Music
comments.content:
Hi John Michael,

I have a theory that I am researching. Do people who are good at learning languages tend to be also good at sight-reading music?

I have noticed the two skills seem to go together. I thought I'd ask here because you seem to be very good at picking up new languages. Do you ever try to read music in standard notation, not "tab" where what's written indicates where to put your fingers? Is it easy for you to read music?

How about everybody else?

Oh, I almost forgot, is magic involved in either of these skills?

Moserian

replies.title: Re: Languages and Sight-Reading Music
replies.content: Yes, I found it very easy to learn to read standard musical notation, though I also use tablature for mountain dulcimer.

replies.title: Re: Languages and Sight-Reading Music
replies.content:
They're not at all linked for me.

Music in its own right is almost a sealed book to me; I can only enjoy it as a "garnish" on some story or other, as, for example, in a traditional ballad or a sea shantey or an opera. And then it is primarily the melody that I can consciously grasp, not so much the rhythms or the timbres or other aspects of the music. Without a story, I much prefer the endlessly varied textures and "shapes" of silence. (This is probably connected to the fact that I am literally unable to think in sentences and narratives; for me, thought is always amatter of mental charts and schemata and mechanisms and systems and outlines. I can, however, translate these things into sentences and narratives to communicate with others, but that takes a noticeable effort.)

Languages, on the other hand, I find delightfully challenging to learn to read, especially dead languages. (I'm a fairly strong introvert, so speaking a language--which involves other people-- has much less appeal tham reading it.) I have lost count of how many languages I have studied, some more deeply, others less so, at various points in my life.

replies.title: Re: Languages and Sight-Reading Music
replies.content:
Sonkitten reads music easily, has a terrible time with foreign languages.

—Princess Cutekitten

replies.title: Re: Languages and Sight-Reading Music
replies.content:
My two cents: Reading music is a perishable skill. You either use it regularly, or you lose it. If a musician practices sight-reading familiar music every day, they will soon be able to read unfamiliar music in their sleep. If they stop practicing that skill, the reflexes for it will RAPIDLY drop off. Other than this dramatic onset of rustiness, I'm not sure how similar these skills are to language-learning.

It might depend on the difficulty level of new material that you're routinely exposed to. If you're reading simple things, you'll be able to recognize familiar sounds/words/phrases and get by... but you will struggle massively with more difficult materials. I guess that's true in both music and language.

Noodles

replies.title: Re: Languages and Sight-Reading Music
replies.content: I sight-read music well, whether standard notation or mountain dulcimer tab, but I think that is because the instrumental music teacher at the elementary school I attended in the late 1960s taught us how to sight-read music so our school band would win the area music competitions. It is a teachable skill. I do not do well at learning foreign languages and have not tried to learn a language since French classes in high school.

comments.title: (no subject)
comments.content:
I recently had a weird experience while watching a documentary about the Titanic: the entire thing felt weirdly familiar; and then when it reached the part about it sinking, I suddenly had a flash of memory: the kind of calm that comes from knowing that your life is over, as I heard a noise, stopped swimming to watch a large ship suddenly tilt and then plunge into the ice cold water I was swimming in. I knew in that moment that it would drag me down with it, but I guess I had gotten far enough away that it did not, because the next part of the memory was shock and then sheer joy that I might yet live. I'm not sure if it was the Titanic, because there were many other ships similar to it that sank into the ocean over the years; but it was unusually vivid and felt pretty much like a memory.

What are the options for figuring out if something is a genuine past life memory or not?

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: Write it down, including every detail, and then put the account away someplace safe. If one memory comes through, others will follow, and will gradually form a coherent image of a life. The one infallible sign that you're dealing with confabulation (aka "making stuff up") rather than real memories is that anything that makes you think you were someone Very Special in a previous life is confabulation. 99.9999% of all souls in incarnation lead ordinary lives.

replies.title: titanic, past lives
replies.content:
To reiterate, my parents told me that I said some things at a very young age while getting into the bath, that indicated that one of my past lives ended on the Titanic. I remember drowning all too well -- the feeling of not being able to hold your breath anymore, instinctively breathing in, only to suck cold water into your lungs, as your consciousness fades out... I'd recommend not drowning if you can help it.
I remember other things too -- there were lights on in the ship, it was cold & dark, and in the open ocean. Of course, the Titanic sunk in the early AM hours, because my past life memories are always corroborated by my research!
As I've said, these images just appeared in my mind, and I didn't even realize what they were at first. Since the memory of drowning is so familiar, it took me awhile to realize that it shouldn't be possible for me to have such a memory, unless there's more to reality than meets the eye.
Aside from memories bubbling up, I've also experienced flashes of recognition, albeit from different lives than that one.

btw, I wonder if we met back then, over a century ago -- it's entirely possible.

replies.title: (no subject)

1) I was 22 when I started serious magical training and began to tackle the steaming mess that was my life. If you start now, even if you're as messed up as I was, you can have your life in order by the time you're 64 and have a happy and productive old age. Consider the alternative; what have you got to lose?

2) Before you do anything else, you need to figure out why you've spent 20 years twiddling your thumbs, when presumably your life hasn't been what you want it to be. No matter what path you choose, it'll accomplish nothing until you approach it with an undivided will -- and if part of you wants to continue wallowing in failure and unhappiness, your chances of doing anything else aren't good.

I'm not mocking you, by the way, or being facetious. Most people who fail at life choose to fail. Very often the choice isn't entirely conscious, but it's always at least partly conscious. (It certainly was for me.) If you want to succeed, you have to understand why you've chosen to fail up to this point, deal with the desires and emotions that underlie that choice, and choose otherwise with all your heart.

3) Mental magic is simply another way of talking about the active side of New Thought. You can turn your life around completely using it. Ceremonial magic is another way of doing the same thing, and it's appropriate for some people and less so for others. I benefited hugely from it, but these days I basically don't use it at all -- I can get comparable results by meditative and New Thought methods.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Winifred here--thank you for the call-out, Jeff! And to set the record straight, it's not only the Norse; in fact, in many ways the Old Saxon Heliand has been the most useful of all the texts. There's also a lot of info from the Anglo-Saxons.

To SDI: one of the things I've hoped could come out of my decades of research on and practice with the Heathen souls is a recognition that the methods I've used could potentially be used to do the same kind of research in other languages and cultures, concerning the nature of the soul(s). It does depend on having sufficient textual material that mentions things about the souls, in the original languages, as well as resources for learning the relevant language(s). I imagine this approach will be quite challenging to do in ancient Egyptian, though!

I talk more about my approach on my website, but in a nutshell here it is:

1) Work on learning the original language(s), especially the nouns, while also reading through the entire Egyptian dictionary to pick out any words that could be relevant to ancient Egyptian concepts of the soul and soul-functions. Don't assume that modern scholars and translators have clearly identified all of them. Based on what you noted in your msg, it sounds like there's more work to be done here!

2) With your list of soul-related terms, scan through texts in the original language(s) to find how they were used in context. I did this long ago before the internet and efiles made this so easy; I skimmed through many hundreds of paper pages, and found a lot of stuff that I would have missed if I'd used word-search functions rather than eyeballs on paper, alert for any clues and side-tracks!

3) Make your own translations of the passages you find, but don't translate the soul-terms, leave them in the original. It's essential to come to understand the soul-terms *untranslated*, learning them the way a child learns its native language: through context.

4) Compare your translations of the passages with those of others, including translations into languages other than English if you know such, like French, etc. You might be able to work around certain language-biases this way. Also, obviously, pay attention to artwork, artifacts, etc, that might offer any clues to the concepts you're working on.

5) Work with this material like a jigsaw puzzle or a Zen koan, to try to get an intuitive grasp of what is being communicated. Chew on it, meditate on it, journal about it, use divination if you like that, seek knowledge through your dreams, and search within yourself to see whether what you are grasping resonates with your own soul(s). Spend a lot of time on this, like years.

5) Then try to find some kindred spirits with whom to compare notes and to work out specific spiritual and psychological practices based on what you've figured out.

That's it, in a nutshell, piece of cake....! Perhaps reading some of the soul lore material on my website will help by offering examples of the approach. It's a lot of work, but it's also a labor of love, and the rewards are life-changing.

Best of luck!
Winifred Hodge Rose

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
That's interesting, that you're working with Greek soul-concepts. I've studied them some, too (I lived for 17 years in Greece, child and adult, and speak modern Greek well, which helps a bit with the ancient--at least I can read the alphabet easily!). There are some amazing similarities as well as differences.

As one example, I see close connections, identity really, between the Greek Aion soul, and the Germanic Aldr. In fact, there are almost identical poetic mentions of these two souls:

In the Beowulf poem, a Geatish spearman shot at a sea-serpent and the spear "stood in the Aldr" of the sea-serpent.

In an Orphic hymn to Hermes he shoots his arrows at his sacred cattle, "piercing them in their Aiones."

Anglo-Saxon has the term Ealdorgeard or Aldr-yard, the body as the enclosure of the Aldr soul, and these poetic descriptions show the idea of the Ealdorgeard being pierced and the Aldr life-soul draining away. Considering that there's about 1600 years or so, and most of a continent of distance between these two cultures, these similarities are pretty amazing!

If you'd like to go to my website using the link Jeff posted and leave a comment about how to contact you, perhaps we could discuss more of this!

Winifred

comments.title: Something vampiric
comments.content:
Last week I posted about my late Grandmother who, as JMG pointed out, was most likely a witch. (https://ecosophia.dreamwidth.org/300033.html?thread=51658497#cmt51658497) I quickly jumped on the advice given and made myself a hoodoo bath and red bag amulet and thought I'd share my results, after a couple follow-up questions.

1.) Would overly rainy weather affect the protective bath? What I mean is, since last week there has been an incredible amount of rain here - some part of my city have outright flooded - and since one aspect of the bath is to pour out the bath water on to the roots of a tree would heavily rain-soaked soil be less effective at absorbing what needs to be taken into the earth?
I'd like to do another round of purifying but I don't know if I should wait until the weather normalizes a bit

2.) As I have reason to believe that an element of the attacks volleyed my way was vampiric in nature, is there anything I should do to address this specifically? One point Dion Fortune mentions about vampiric attacks, "vampirism is contagious; the person who is vampirised, being depleted of vitality, is a psychic vacuum, himself absorbing from anyone he comes across in order to refill his depleted resources of vitality." is both relevant and...concerning.




Results:

First, despite the temperature of the bath being "as cool as you can handle", I didn't feel chilled by the water, and my feet didn't go numb which they are very prone to doing. Neat!

Second, and most significantly, my dreams have changed. The most obvious of the changes has been the sudden appearance of helpful people. When something unpleasant occurs to me now, (which is happening less frequently!) somebody will show up to lend me help, come to my defence or interfere on my behalf. This is huge! 🤯

As a small example: I've had nightmares that centre around elevators my whole life. After getting into one and starting to move between floors, they would suddenly malfunction in very particular ways - moving in directions they shouldn't, taking off and plummeting at terrifying speeds, that sort of thing. In a recent dream (in which I was attempting to flee a building), a voice starting telling me to pay attention to the physical/bodily sensations I was experiencing, "this is how you can tell when there is an unwanted presence around you." When the elevator doors opened up I could immediately see that there was everything wrong with it, so I chose the stairs instead! For the very first time I avoided the elevator! I could cry with how relieved I am about this


and Thirdly, what I think the attacks were doing, and why I believe vampirism was an element: After spending some time meditating on the changes in my dreams it was very clear that there were two prominent aspects: A.) All manner of ghoulies and nasties seemed to have unfettered access my psychic space, and B.) kind and helpful beings were somehow barred entrance. I got the impression that my astral environment was like a house that had all the doors taken off the hinges, the windows smashed open, and a "come on in!" sign plastered on the side. This lead it to being such a breeding ground for everything unpleasant that the atmosphere around it was too noxious for anything else to penetrate. I suspect at least some of the activity of these unwanted visitors had to do with them feeding, as some of the most disturbing nightmares I've had over my lifetime all involved strange men appearing out of nowhere to tear apart and consume still living animals...an truly wretched spectacle.
Any proof I needed about the nature of my Grandmother was found when I read these lines from Dion's Psychic Self Defence, "the last that was heard of Miss X [a suspected vampiric witch] was that she had been removed to a nursing-home in the country with a severe mental breakdown." a breakdown "which followed immediately upon [her victims] deaths." A couple of years after her husband passed away, my younger cousin (who still lived in the same area as grandma) suddenly committed suicide. Not long after this, grandma began to suffer from increasingly paranoid delusions. In a call with my dad he mentioned "She has all kinds of things hanging from the doors because she's convinced herself people are going to break in". She also stopped eating enough food to maintain her health. These two things became severe enough that she had to be removed from her home and put into care. hmmm 🤔

Perhaps she had some kind of deal with some contacts where she would tear down the psychic defences of people she had relationships/rapport with in exchange for benefits and/or protection? Perhaps after she lost access to any potential victims her contacts then came for her?
A chilling thought

comments.title: Halloween on the eld of the moon
comments.content: Is there any occult significance to Halloween occurring on the eld of the moon?

replies.title: Re: Halloween on the eld of the moon
replies.content:
Despite a great deal of talk by fundamentalists Christians and Wiccans, Halloween isn't of any particular occult importance; its relevance is purely that it's the eve of an old Christian holy day, All Saints' Day -- in older English, "All Hallows' Day," which is where we get "All Hallows' Eve," "Hallow E'en," and the current name. So the only implication of Halloween in the eld of the Moon is that it'll probably be quieter than most years.

That said, no doubt politically minded Wiccans will be out there casting death spells against Donald Trump, now that so many of them have abandoned the whole "witches heal" schtick and gone whole hog into evil magic and demonolatry. I don't expect them to be any more competent than usual, but there's likely to be a fair amount of creepy energy about. An extra banishing or two might not go amiss.

replies.title: Re: Halloween on the eld of the moon
replies.content: If I may piggyback on this: my impression is that Halloween evolved not only out of All Hallow's Day, but also Samhain, the Celtic festival in which the boundary between spirits and the mundane became porous, or so it was believed. That to me sounds like it would relate to the occult, unless I'm mistaken.

replies.title: Re: Halloween on the eld of the moon
replies.content: If you happen to be an ancient Celt, sure.

replies.title: Re: Halloween on the eld of the moon
replies.content: Lol, so I guess that no longer applies. Maybe they had a spiritual connection that we don't?

replies.title: Re: Halloween on the eld of the moon
replies.content: It's more subtle than that. Human cultures can lay down tracks in space to limit certain kinds of phenomena to specific periods of time, and that can be very important in the aftermath of eras where a lot of human sacrifice and evil magic is practiced. (That's why the Day of the Dead is so important in Mexico these days; they're clearing up the magical aftermath of the Aztec worship of Huitzilopochtli.) Once the culture fades out, though, so do the tracks.

replies.title: Re: Halloween on the eld of the moon
replies.content: Fascinating stuff, thanks. Magic is like another continent that I've only recently started exploring.

comments.title: Recovery aids
comments.content: Please bear with me and I hope this is not seen as a silly question. I had to undergo major surgery 4 weeks ago and as part of the recovery, to prevent a deep vein thrombosis  I have to wear compression socks for 6 weeks. I have developed a love/hate relationship with the things. They are very uncomfortable and a pain to wear day in day out. But they might, just might, have saved my life. My question is at the end of six weeks is there a good way,speaking in either occult or natural magic terms, to dispense them? Since starting my occult journey I have found a kind of animist gnosis pervading me almost by stealth. I can't but think of everything being alive, the dratted (blessed) socks included. replies.title: Re: Recovery aids
replies.content:
Clean ones can be donated, place a note with the size and compression rating with them.

I wear light compression socks (15 - 20 mm) every day, they are much more comfortable and easy to put on than the heavy medical 30 - 40 mm ones. Packs with six to eight pairs of them with festive patterns are inexpensive from the big muddy river. They do stink to high heaven at the end of the day, and you can't put them in the dryer, but at the end of every day my legs thank me.

replies.title: Re: Recovery aids

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
Have you read Metropolitan Tikhon's *Everyday Saints*?

I'm halfway through it now, and there's a story in there that's reminiscent of what you've related here. It's titled *Father Melchisedek*, and relates the story of a monk who was a woodworker and made all sorts of ornate carved liturgical furniture. He has a near-death experience, and then asks to be released from woodworking forever, and become a schema-monk. The abbot granted his request.

Why?

While he was out (and thought to be dead), he'd visited a place, he said, with all sorts of misshapen, clunky bits of furniture and woodcarving, half in mud. His own carpentry. The Mother of God spoke to him, sadly, and told him "You're a monk... And all we wanted from you was just one thing, the main thing: repentance and prayer. Instead of that, you gave us this woodwork..."

FWIW, I had a bit of an identity crisis myself, the many years ago after I became Orthodox, and then married and had kids. Before that, I had done all sorts of artwork and handcrafting-- it was kind of like breathing. Just *did* stuff. Sewed neat things that I gave away. Painted very intricate designs on furniture. Did a lot of embroidery, drawing, photography, that sort of thing. The odd paid commission here and there. I liked making beautiful things. And then... kids. So much of that depended on having long stretches of quiet, uninterrupted time, which didn't happen anymore. My eldest is 12 now, and I recently got out the old pencil box to do a drawing exercise with them (yeah, we homeschool), and the kids were like: "Mama! I didn't know you could do anything that good!" (sigh)

I miss it. Not the *things* but the meditative, zoned-out, flow-state process of *doing* that stuff. It's not possible where I'm never getting enough sleep, and my waking hours are interrupted every 30 seconds by a small person who *needs* something-- a boo-boo kissed, help on an algebra problem, a drink of water, a dispute resolved, and the other million things that cannot wait another ten minutes. It's getting better, as older kids can sometimes help little one with some of these things, but still...

The thing is, as much as I miss that *mental space*-- I think it's the same channel we use for prayer and meditation, and lately I wonder if, part of the necessary work of conversion, is learning how to use that channel properly, how to integrate it, when I'm so accustomed to using it for... basically selfish purposes. I think there's a prioritizing that has to happen. The prayer and meditation have to come first, and then if any of the creative stuff comes (and it needn't), it wants to be after, downstream, of that. I don't know yet. I'm still trying to figure it out. In the meantime, maybe part of it is that God gave us children to raise, and some of that creative capacity is owed to them.

One spot where, sometimes clumsily, things come together, has been learning to chant. I'm not good at it, and actually chanting at church is the low point of the thing: it's a job, and I do it because somebody has to. Singing in front of other people retains a sort of lifelong horror it's had since childhood for me. But the *learning* part... that's very different. Putting in a half hour, an hour, here and there, out in the garage after the kids are in bed, tackling the texts, has brought me back to that old mental space, that I missed. But now it is part and parcel of the prayer and meditation. I talk to the saint whose apolytikion I am learning: please pray for me, so that I can learn this well enough to do it with proper respect and not make a hash of it! Help me trace the right path through this score, so them that hear, are led through you to God, not noticing me on the way. The memorizing of things has been interesting, in the way that a psalm or a kathismata, sung fifty or a hundred times, begins to meld with my psyche and become a permanent fixture. Bits of them surface at opportune times: "Keep me from the snares which they have laid for me..." and "Will you ever call me your disciple anymore, or will you again appoint me a fisher of the deep?" and "make this raiment of my soul brightly shine..."

Which is a long-winded way of saying: I don't really have the One Big Answer for you, and I'm not a person to take advice from, but perhaps... it's a thing worth meditating on ;) And sometimes, it's just a season for other kinds of work, and kids are certainly other work. Maybe as they get older I'll be able to revisit that stuff. Maybe I'm not that person anymore. Maybe I'll be called some other direction. Maybe there is mundane work that's more important: toilets still have to be scrubbed, and dishes washed. But I still have a lot of work to do, tending the garden of my soul... and whatever happens, that has to be the top priority.

I just realized, though, that the same part of me I hate, is also the part that holds the key to achieving my goals. Now I struggle with either using this aspect of my personality towards irrational ends, or loathing myself for using it sometimes inappropriately and intentionally avoiding the use of that energy - which is now producing unwanted results as well.

When you have an aspect of your personality that is both a strength and weakness, and you vary from naively embracing it to consciously loathing it and avoiding it - how can one find the means to find that balance?

I realize part of my problem is I just need to accept my shadow is part of me. It needs to be transformed ultimately - the shadow I see in me is an archetype you don't want to be stuck playing the part of - but hating yourself because you have that shadow in you clearly doesn't solve the problem.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content: All aspects of personality are both strengths and weaknesses. We simply tend to notice one or the other side of them, and misjudge them accordingly. Remember that the word "personality" comes from the Latin word for "mask." That's all the personality is -- a temporary mask over your real self, the self that endures from life to life. It's not you; to put things another way, it's simply a set of habits of perception and action that you've taken up. Accept that your personality contains a buffet of strengths and weaknesses, and that all of these are inescapably present to you and can be useful in their place, and you can make good use of it.

comments.title: Involuntary Glamour and De-glamourization

1) From time to time there's talking about "glamour (spells)" on these pages. From what I've read I gather that in this context, "glamour" means somebody (or something) appearing more appealing to others than the inner and outer qualities of the person or thing would suggest. Is it possible that parents unconsciously cast a glamour spell on their children? Judging from my own experience (as a child) this could well be true. For some years now I experience what I would describe as "de-glamouring". Major contributors to this were the cold realizations which originated from the C19-debacle and active work with both my own past and that of my family. But probably also taking up the SOP - can a regular practice of the SOP contribute to getting rid of "glamour"? As a result, I am much more withdrawn from society and it has become much easier for me to say no to things or generally dodge unwanted tasks. Both is rather welcome.

2) It's possible to enforce one's speech and actions with astral imagery - and "glamour". On the other hand - if for example you don't allow this imagery to play out during talking to somebody or if you have cast off glamour attached to you, you may come over very unconvincing to others and your opinion tends to be ignored in discussions at least when other participants display those qualities more strongly than you. At least that's my experience. I wondered if it is possible to use this effect consciously - for example when you are forced to talk about things that you don't want to talk about? When I say "we should be precise in our prayers" I mean that - for example - if you pray for help it could be sensible to describe the problem you need help with as good as you can (which in itself contributes to finding a solution quite often).

Nachtgurke

comments.title: Pets and the Aferlife
comments.content:
Dear JMG,
I have a mischievous loving 6-year-old mini Poodle named Friar, and I can't imagine the Beyond devoid of him. What is your views on pets' part in the afterlife. Do they maintain an individual (vs collective) soul or identity? And what forms would relationship between human and special pet take? Also, are we helping them evolve, during earth life and/or in the Between-Life, toward the human kingdom?

Many Thanks as Always!

replies.title: Re: Pets and the Aferlife
replies.content:
Animals have individual souls that reincarnate; the group soul they also have is simply there to provide them with the guidance we're supposed to get from our intellects, but usually ignore. If Friar passes before you do, he'll almost certainly be there waiting when you die, along with deceased human friends and relatives. After that you'll both have work to do, processing your previous life, before reincarnating.

Animals that are very close to humans are usually in their last life before becoming human. It's quite possible that in your next life Friar may be your human child or sibling -- that kind of close relationship is extremely beneficial to a soul that's just reached the human level. More generally, it's quite rare for any close relationship to last for only one life, so you don't have to worry.

replies.title: (no subject)
replies.content:
It's not a matter of the apparent movement around the earth -- it's a matter of the apparent movement of the sun against the backdrop of the stars. On any given day the sun appears to be carried around the earth with the rest of the starry heavens. Each day, though, it appears to shift slightly in relation to the stars.

Thus it doesn't matter what time of day it is tomorrow, for example, the sun will be located right where the wedge of the ecliptic labeled "Libra" gives way to the one labeled "Scorpio." From our earthly perspective, that point on the ecliptic rises, culminates, and sets along with the sun. It's just that the next day, the sun will have moved one degree further.

Does that help clarify things a little?

No.

The standard rule is that if you have a majority of favorable figures, that's a yes, and if you have a majority of unfavorable figures, it's a no. Tristitia and Cauda Draconis are two of the three most unfavorable figures in geomancy, and Via is neutral. If you go, you'll be unhappy there and the arrangement won't last.

comments.title: Paedrig's Pentacles
comments.content:
Hello! So I recently tried to help clear some bad energy from my friend's home - they had just evicted a tenant that... well, to say they were troubled would be an understatement. My friend found them living on the street and tried to help them straighten out and function. I'm almost certain they were haunted by some sort of malevolent spirit. I could sense it, whenever I was near, and my friend swore he sensed the same thing.

Anyway, after one too many relapses and meltdowns, the guy finally got evicted. I figured it would be good to try and clear the air - I'm not trying to cast out a spirit, which I wish I could have done for this guy, but I knew it was probably bound to him for a reason and doing that is beyond my abilities. I just wanted to remove the miasma, the residue left over. Basic spiritual cleaning, that sort of thing.

It turned out to be far deeper and darker than I expected - something unsettling was set loose. Maybe a piece of the original spirit, or offspring? Everything seemed fine at first - the room had been cleaned, set in order, it looked brand new. However, my friend seemed sick, like he was having trouble breathing. His health has deteriorated since he took in the haunted man. I just lit a stick of sage in an old pot while we got to work on my vehicle - we hang out and try to keep old cars running, sometimes playing music together.

The sage started smoking voluminously, and when I brought the pot out around my friend, he started coughing, so outside it went. There seemed to be something in the smoke - a swirling whirlwind, moving, almost alive. I said some prayers, banishing the darkness and miasma and all malevolent, parasitic spirits. I use my talisman to Jupiter, claiming its nor fair or just for my friend, who was nothing but kind and generous, to be cursed for his kindness in this way. I kind of feel like a talisman to saturn might have worked better for banishing. My saturn is not badly aspected in my chart, so I might try and work with him next.

In any case, when I brought the sage back into the room, it smoked up worse than a cheech and chong movie, and I felt something writhing and whirling around, like I had seriously disturbed something. At the end, I got my friend to join me in a prayer to Jesus that whatever dark energy or spirits had come into the house with the haunted guest leave immediately and never return. I left the sage burning in the pot in the room and departed.

Well, I tell you what, I think the being left... I think it left with me. I felt strange all day, everything was... disjointed, unreal, confusing, I dont know. I felt contaminated and afraid. I was afraid to be around my children even, and I still worry if I passed some kind of spiritual miasma on to them - they have had headaches lately... I prayed for any miasma around them to move from them to me to me, and visualized it happening.

As soon as they were asleep, I went outside to my circle and did the sphere of protection ritual. I felt the energies flowing through me more intensely than I ever had, and each eleemental expulsion, each flow of energy seemed to clear away a flood of dark pollution. As soon as the ritual was done, I felt like a weight was lifted from my soul. Like I had cast away something truly malevolent. I wemt insidr, and went to sleep sound as a baby.

The thing is, I found out from a friend that an old shack I used to live in - a very haunted, almost cursed place, with a civil war mass grave in the back - had burned down that night. Its now a shell of its former self. I cant help but wonder if these things are connected...

I think I'm jist going to focus on regular sphere of protection rituals for the foreseeable future, along with the southern conjure cleansing bath you recommend in your faqs, maybe the protective amulet mentioned there, too.

Any other advice or insights into my experience? Anything I should have dome differently, tips for the future, leasons to meditate on? As always, I appreciate your guidance and help.

comments.title: Ether
comments.content:
I've been reading through the ebook version of Polarity Magic, and even though I haven't finished it I think it's well worth recommending.

Just one small side point (these things always happen!). Ether (more specifically, diethyl ether, one of a larger class of ethers) is not nitrous oxide. Ether is an organic compound alchemically produced by mixing ethanol with sulphuric acid. (There are other ways of producing it more commonly used nowadays.) It much pre-dates nitrous oxide, which is an inorganic compound (N20).

Not that I suppose an erratum slip will be tipped in to the hard copies before they are sent out. But is there is another edition, or another printing, this might be a useful little rebision.

Smaragdopolitan senex
